content:
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed and uncertain about your relationship. I want to acknowledge that it takes a lot of courage to admit when things aren't working out.

If you feel safe, talking to your husband about your concerns and feelings can be a great first step. It might help to identify the specific issues that are causing you distress and work together to find a resolution.

Alternatively, seeking support from a trusted friend, family member, or mental health professional can provide a safe and non-judgmental space to explore your emotions and develop a plan for moving forward.

If you're considering ending the relationship, prioritize your own well-being and take care of yourself during this time. This might involve connecting with loved ones, engaging in self-care activities, and exploring local resources for support.

Lastly, consider reaching out to organizations that specialize in supporting individuals in similar situations, such as the National Domestic Violence Hotline (1-800-799-7233) or the National Coalition Against Domestic Violence (NCADV). They can offer guidance, resources, and emotional support.

Remember, you deserve to feel safe, respected, and loved in your relationship.
